feature: Inventory and dependency graph: Clusters / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on Clusters cost posture without leaving Mayyam.

cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for Clusters, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.

dependency: Clusters connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate Brokers security issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for Brokers.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for Brokers.
data_model: brokers_security_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Brokers security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Brokers; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Brokers.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Brokers recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Brokers.
acceptance_criteria: Given Brokers data exists, when the security agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Brokers connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for Broker disk performance posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for Broker disk.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for Broker disk.
data_model: broker-disk_performance_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Broker disk performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Broker disk; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Broker disk.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Broker disk recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Broker disk.
acceptance_criteria: Given Broker disk data exists, when the performance automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Broker disk connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
